title: Collision resolution

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Collision resolution; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Algorithmic Foundations; August 2023; material applicable to Collision resolution
sources.claim: Collision resolution uses separate chaining or open addressing to distinguish unequal keys sharing a bucket.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Collision resolution uses separate chaining or open addressing to distinguish unequal keys sharing a bucket.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Linear probing continues until the key or a truly empty slot, not merely the first deleted slot, is resolved. Your goal is to trace insert, find and delete through collisions in a teaching hash table. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Array representation and operations; Linked lists; Hash and equality contract; Singly linked representation. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: Collision resolution uses separate chaining or open addressing to distinguish unequal keys sharing a bucket. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: In C, implement or select a table with explicit hash, equality, capacity, and ownership; in C++, unordered containers require compatible hash/equality and stable keys; in Java, HashMap/HashSet depend on equals and hashCode; in Python, dict/set require hashable keys with consistent __eq__ and __hash__.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Probe deletion needs tombstones, clustering affects cost, and adversarial hashes can degrade expected constant time. The draft therefore rejects: Two different keys with one hash value overwrite each other automatically.

includes: Explain chaining and open addressing with load factor, probing and clustering trade-offs.
Trace insert, find and delete through collisions in a teaching hash table.

In C, implement or select a table with explicit hash, equality, capacity, and ownership; in C++, unordered containers require compatible hash/equality and stable keys; in Java, HashMap/HashSet depend on equals and hashCode; in Python, dict/set require hashable keys with consistent __eq__ and __hash__.
excludes: Probe deletion needs tombstones, clustering affects cost, and adversarial hashes can degrade expected constant time.

outcomes: Trace insert, find and delete through collisions in a teaching hash table.

examples.title: Collision resolution: language-neutral trace
examples.situation: Linear probing continues until the key or a truly empty slot, not merely the first deleted slot, is resolved.
walkthrough: Step 1 — State the abstract input and invariant: Linear probing continues until the key or a truly empty slot, not merely the first deleted slot, is resolved.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: A collision strategy stores and compares colliding keys rather than treating the hash as unique identity.
examples.result: The conceptual result follows because A collision strategy stores and compares colliding keys rather than treating the hash as unique identity.

examples.title: Collision resolution: implementation transfer
examples.situation: In C, implement or select a table with explicit hash, equality, capacity, and ownership; in C++, unordered containers require compatible hash/equality and stable keys; in Java, HashMap/HashSet depend on equals and hashCode; in Python, dict/set require hashable keys with consistent __eq__ and __hash__.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Keep the same specification and invariant across all four languages.

Step 2 — Map storage, library, equality, bounds, ownership, and error rules using: In C, implement or select a table with explicit hash, equality, capacity, and ownership; in C++, unordered containers require compatible hash/equality and stable keys; in Java, HashMap/HashSet depend on equals and hashCode; in Python, dict/set require hashable keys with consistent __eq__ and __hash__.
Step 3 — Retest this boundary in the selected implementation: Probe deletion needs tombstones, clustering affects cost, and adversarial hashes can degrade expected constant time.
examples.result: Concept mastery transfers, while implementation evidence remains language-specific.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: Two different keys with one hash value overwrite each other automatically.
misconceptions.correction: A collision strategy stores and compares colliding keys rather than treating the hash as unique identity.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use an invariant or counterexample to explain why this DSA belief fails: Two different keys with one hash value overwrite each other automatically.

analogy.example: A carefully labeled physical arrangement can introduce Collision resolution, while the canonical invariant and operations remain the actual algorithmic model.
usefulFor: Introducing structure, order, connectivity, or state transition before formal notation
limitations: A physical arrangement does not reproduce asymptotic cost, aliasing, numeric limits, recursion depth, or the four languages' implementation rules.
Probe deletion needs tombstones, clustering affects cost, and adversarial hashes can degrade expected constant time.

recap.summary: You should now be able to trace insert, find and delete through collisions in a teaching hash table. Keep this boundary in view: Probe deletion needs tombstones, clustering affects cost, and adversarial hashes can degrade expected constant time.
